urgency.message: 3 critical CVE(s) and 5 new KEV addition(s) in the last 7 days. Review immediately.

items.title: Threat Brief: Active Exploitation of PAN-OS CVE-2026-0257
items.summary:
We include indicators of activity and mitigations for PAN-OS vulnerability CVE-2026-0257.
The post Threat Brief: Active Exploitation of PAN-OS CVE-2026-0257 appeared first on Unit 42.

items.title: What 2026 DBIR Confirms: Attacks Are Living in the Browser
items.summary: Phishing, shadow AI, malicious extensions, and credential theft increasingly happen inside the browser. Keep Aware explains what the 2026 Verizon DBIR reveals about browser-layer security gaps and modern attacks. [...]

items.title: CVE-2026-33841 Windows Kernel Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability
items.summary: Updated an acknowledgement. This is an informational change only.

items.title: In Other News: Anthropic Maps AI Threats, Unpatched Comodo Flaw, Palantir Chief Eyed for CISA
items.summary:

items.title: The Good, the Bad and the Ugly in Cybersecurity – Week 23
items.summary: US Treasury sanctions Iran's largest crypto exchange, PRC-linked TA4922 expands phishing to Europe and Africa, attackers exploit Palo Alto VPN bypass.

items.title: Trump AI Order Seeks Voluntary Frontier Model Testing
items.summary: The White House's executive order establishes voluntary framework for early government access to frontier models while investing in federal security.

items.title: New Threat Cluster OP-512 Targets Microsoft IIS Servers with Custom Web Shell Framework
items.summary:
Cybersecurity researchers have discovered a previously unreported threat cluster dubbed OP-512 that has been observed targeting Microsoft Internet Information Services (IIS) servers to deploy a bespoke web shell framework.

ReliaQuest has assessed with moderate to high confidence that the espionage-

items.summary: The cybersecurity industry has spent much of the last two years debating how attackers might use AI. items.title: Hackers Leak DentaQuest Information Impacting 2.6 Million
items.summary:
The ShinyHunters extortion group leaked roughly 234 GB of data allegedly stolen from the dental benefits administrator.
The post Hackers Leak DentaQuest Information Impacting 2.6 Million appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Only 10% of SOCs Say They’re Getting Excellent Value From AI. Here’s What the Second Wave Has to Deliver
items.summary: Eighteen months ago, the AI SOC was a marketing line. Today it's a budget item. The category has crossed over from interesting to inevitable, with billions of dollars now flowing into AI-powered security operations platforms, agentic SOC tools, and AI co-pilots built into every layer of the security

items.title: Chrome 149 Patches 429 Vulnerabilities
items.summary:
Over 100 bugs are critical or high-severity, mainly use-after-free and insufficient validation of untrusted input flaws.
The post Chrome 149 Patches 429 Vulnerabilities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Industry Reactions to New Trump AI Cybersecurity Executive Order: Feedback Friday
items.summary:
Experts commented on the EO’s voluntary nature, the balance between innovation and security, and potential implementation gaps.
The post Industry Reactions to New Trump AI Cybersecurity Executive Order: Feedback Friday appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Five Eyes: Chinese Spies Target Government, Military Staff With Fake Job Opportunities
items.summary:
Posing as recruiters on online platforms, Chinese intelligence officers target personnel with access to classified or privileged information.
The post Five Eyes: Chinese Spies Target Government, Military Staff With Fake Job Opportunities appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Hackers Exploit Critical Everest Forms Pro WordPress Plugin Flaw to Take Over Sites
items.summary:
Threat actors are actively exploiting a critical security flaw in Everest Forms Pro, a WordPress plugin with about 4,000 active installations, to execute arbitrary code, leading to a complete site compromise.

The vulnerability in question is CVE-2026-3300 (CVSS score: 9.8), a remote code execution

items.title: Nightclub Giant RCI Says Data Breach Affects 40,000 Individuals
items.summary:
The company detected a network intrusion in March and an investigation showed that some files were stolen during the attack.
The post Nightclub Giant RCI Says Data Breach Affects 40,000 Individuals appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Scams Are Already Live: Fake Sites, Banking Malware, and Stolen Logins
items.summary:
Security researchers and the FBI are warning that a wave of FIFA-themed fraud is already hitting World Cup 2026 fans, days before the June 11 kickoff.

items.title: The Evil MSI Background is Back!, (Fri, Jun 5th)
items.summary: A few months ago, I wrote a diary about a payload that was embedded into a JPEG picture. It was a MSI-branded background[1]. Yesterday, I spotted another one! It seems that the technic is getting more and more popular. This time, it started with a mail containing a WeTransfer link.&#x

items.title: Cisco warns of unpatched SD-WAN zero-day exploited in attacks
items.summary: On Thursday, Cisco warned of a high-severity, unpatched zero-day in the Cisco Catalyst SD-WAN Manager (tracked as CVE-2026-20245) actively exploited in attacks enabling root privilege escalation. [...]

items.title: Cisco Warns of 7th SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited in 2026
items.summary:
The vulnerability is tracked as CVE-2026-20245 and it can allow arbitrary command execution as root, but no patch yet.
The post Cisco Warns of 7th SD-WAN Zero-Day Exploited in 2026 appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: PCPJack Hijacks 230 AWS, Google Cloud, and Azure Servers for Covert SMTP Relay Network
items.summary:
The threat actor known as PCPJack has hijacked cloud servers associated with Amazon Web Services (AWS), Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure to create a covert SMTP email relay network.

"Compromised business servers across the U.S., Europe, and Asia were quietly converted into SMTP proxies, verified f

items.summary:

items.title: Rust-Written IronWorm Hits NPM Supply Chain
items.summary: Like Shai-Hulud, the campaign targets developers to steal credentials and reuses them to propagate across the software supply channel.

items.title: Brave Software releases Origin for a paid, bloat-free browsing experience
items.summary: Brave has announced the public release of Brave Origin, a paid minimalist version of its browser that strips out cryptocurrency, AI, rewards, and other monetization-focused features. [...]

items.title: Hola Browser for Windows compromised to deliver cryptominer
items.summary: The Windows version of the Hola Browser has been compromised in a supply chain attack that delivered an undeclared executable identified by researchers as a cryptocurrency miner. [...]

items.title: China's TA4922 Expands Cybercrime Attacks Globally
items.summary: One of the world's most diverse, least-focused cybercrime groups is enlarging its footprint beyond East Asia.

items.title: 4 Critical Threats Where Attackers Have the Advantage
items.summary: Gartner analysts issued a call to action to bolster defenses against several emerging critical threats, such as deepfakes and prompt injections.

items.title: Credit card theft campaign abuses Stripe to host stolen payment info
items.summary: A new Magecart campaign is using Stripe's API infrastructure to host the credit card-stealing payload and the data exfiltrated from checkout pages. [...]

items.summary: By participating in Project Glasswing and working with Claude Mythos Preview, Tenable can help customers better understand how emerging frontier AI models behave, their evolving risks and benefits for cybersecurity, and the kinds of controls organizations will need as AI adoption accelerates.Key tak

items.title: DentaQuest data breach  exposed info of 2.6 million accounts
items.summary: A data breach at the dental benefits administrator DentaQuest has reportedly exposed the sensitive data of 2.6 million accounts.  [...]

items.title: Reporting from Vegas: Networking, AI, and good boys
items.summary: Joe’s on-the-ground report from Cisco Live U.S. is here, complete with therapy dog pictures and tips on handling conference overstimulation.

items.title: Meta Silently Added Face-Recognition Code for Its Smart Glasses to Millions of Phones
items.summary: Code reviewed by WIRED uncovered an unreleased face-recognition system embedded in Meta’s smart glasses platform. It’s designed to identify people via biometric data stored on users’ phones.

items.title: Cisco Patches CVE-2026-20230 in Unified CM as Exploit Code Goes Public
items.summary:
Cisco has patched a bug in Unified Communications Manager that lets an unauthenticated attacker on the network write files to the box and, from there, climb to root.

It is tracked as CVE-2026-20230, and proof-of-concept exploit code is already public. Cisco's PSIRT says it has not seen the flaw use

items.title: UN food agency discloses breach affecting 600,000 Gaza households
items.summary: The United Nations' World Food Programme (WFP), the world's largest humanitarian organization, revealed over the weekend that its self-registration application (SRA) for Palestine was breached. [...]

items.title: New IronWorm malware hits 36 packages in npm supply-chain attack
items.summary: A new supply-chain attack has infected 36 packages on the Node Package Manager (npm) index with infostealer malware called IronWorm. [...]

items.title: Claude Code GitHub Action Flaw Let One Malicious Issue Hijack Repositories
items.summary: A security researcher found a flaw in Anthropic's Claude Code GitHub Action that let an attacker take over vulnerable public repositories running it, with nothing more than a single opened GitHub issue. Because Anthropic's own action repo used the same workflow, a working attack could have pushed ma

items.summary: Over the past several weeks, the cybersecurity community has been reminded how quickly frontier and agentic AI in defense networks can challenge our assumptions. items.title: Offroad Emerges From Stealth With $7 Million to Tackle Enterprise Identity Risk
items.summary:
As AI agents, machine identities, and third-party applications multiply across enterprises, Offroad is betting autonomous security agents can restore control over an increasingly unmanageable identity landscape.
The post Offroad Emerges From Stealth With $7 Million to Tackle Enterprise Identity Risk

items.title: Bugcrowd Launches EU Data Residency Option For Evolving Data Sovereignty Needs
items.summary: Organizations are growing serious about what nation’s rules apply to their data. Experts point to geopolitical tensions as a main contributing factor.

items.title: CVE-2026-48567 Azure HorizonDB  Elevation of Privilege Vulnerability
items.summary: Authentication bypass by spoofing in Azure HorizonDB allows an unauthorized attacker to elevate privileges over a network.

items.title: Winning the cyber marathon with Tony Giandomenico
items.summary: Tony Giandomenico, Senior Director of Product Management, joins Amy to discuss the Talos Threat Hunting launch what he's excited about for the future of cybersecurity, and, of course, his Ironman triathlons.
